title: Passive Voice
description: Focus on actions rather than who does them using be + past participle across different tenses.

items.example: The letter is written. The cake was eaten. It will be delivered.
These cars are made in Germany. Spanish is spoken here.
The pyramids were built thousands of years ago.

items.front: Future passive: will be + past participle
items.back: The report will be sent tomorrow.
items.example: Your order will be delivered by Friday.

items.back: It was designed by a famous architect.
items.example: The novel was written by Gabriel García Márquez.

items.front: When to use passive: agent unknown / unimportant / obvious
items.back: My car was stolen. (we don't know who)
items.example: The window was broken. Oil was discovered in the region.

items.front: Perfect passive: have/has been + past participle
items.example: The problem has been solved. Three new species have been discovered.

items.front: Modal passive: modal + be + past participle
items.back: It should be done. / It must be checked.
items.example: All forms must be completed before the deadline.

items.prompt: This famous building ___ by a renowned Spanish architect in 1882.
items.explanation: Past action with the agent mentioned → past passive: was designed by…

items.prompt: English ___ in many countries around the world.
items.explanation: General present fact → present passive: is spoken. English is spoken = people speak English.

items.prompt: The package ___ by Friday, according to the tracking information.
items.explanation: Future passive: will be delivered. The package is the subject receiving the action.

items.prompt: My phone ___ at the café yesterday. I have no idea who took it.
items.explanation: Past event, agent unknown → past passive: was stolen. Classic passive use.

items.prompt: The new policy ___ already ___ by the board of directors.
items.explanation: Perfect passive: has been approved. The policy was approved and is now relevant.

items.sentence: Coffee ___ in Brazil, Colombia, and Ethiopia. (grow)
The Eiffel Tower ___ in 1889 by Gustave Eiffel. (build)
Your application ___ within five working days. (process)
All safety checks ___ before the plane takes off. (must / complete)
Three new planets ___ recently ___ by astronomers. (discover)
